texts.content:
Community Center — Summer Workshop Schedule

July 10–14: Digital Photography for Beginners (9 AM – 11 AM, Room 201)
July 17–21: Introduction to Creative Writing (1 PM – 3 PM, Room 105)
July 24–28: Basic Web Design (9 AM – 12 PM, Computer Lab)
August 7–11: Yoga and Mindfulness (7 AM – 8 AM, Main Hall)

All workshops require advance registration. Registration opens two weeks before each start date. Materials are included except for the photography workshop, where participants must bring their own camera. Class sizes are limited to 15 participants.

questions.stem: Which workshop has the longest daily session time?
options: A. Digital Photography for Beginners
B. Introduction to Creative Writing
C. Basic Web Design
D. Yoga and Mindfulness
questions.explanation: Basic Web Design runs from 9 AM to 12 PM (3 hours), which is longer than the other workshops.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the photography workshop?
options: A. It is the most popular workshop offered
B. It requires an additional equipment fee
C. It will take place in the computer lab
D. It has a separate registration process
questions.explanation: Since participants 'must bring their own camera' and materials are included for other workshops, the photography workshop likely involves an additional equipment cost that participants must cover themselves.

questions.stem: When can a participant register for the yoga workshop?
options: A. Any time before July 24
B. Starting July 24
C. Starting August 7
D. Anytime during the summer
questions.explanation: Registration opens two weeks before each start date. The yoga workshop starts August 7, so registration opens July 24.

texts.content:
BookReviewClub @ The Reading Nook

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The Unlikely Traveler" by K. Morrison

Just finished this novel and I cannot recommend it enough! The characters are richly developed and the plot twists genuinely surprised me. The descriptions of the Southeast Asian settings are so vivid that I felt like I was traveling alongside the protagonist.

Has anyone else read this? Would love to discuss the ending — especially what happened in chapter 18!

#BookReview #Fiction #TravelNovel

questions.stem: What is the reviewer's overall opinion of the book?
options: A. The writing style is too descriptive
B. The plot was predictable
C. The book is highly enjoyable and well-written
D. The characters needed more development
questions.explanation: The reviewer gives five stars and describes the characters as 'richly developed' and the plot twists as 'genuinely surprising,' indicating strong approval.

questions.stem: What does the reviewer imply by mentioning chapter 18?
options: A. The book becomes boring after that chapter
B. The ending was disappointing
C. There is a significant event in that chapter worth discussing
D. The reviewer did not finish reading the book
questions.explanation: The reviewer says they 'would love to discuss the ending — especially what happened in chapter 18,' implying that chapter contains a key plot development.
